Title: Consider parsing a subset of the Taskwarrior expression syntax and grammar
Body:
There are some existing issues about providing logical OR (https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/112), as well as some that consider doing this via existing Taskwarrior features that are usually set in `TASKRC` (https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/116, https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/108, https://github.com/bgregos/foreground/issues/49).

I won't attempt to illustrate what kinds of expressions users will expect to use in Foreground. Others might have comment.

I did want to make this request explicit, as it seems to be currently implicitly alluded to in a few other issues. The challenges or strategies might begin to be outlined here.

To get a sense of the problem, we might consider the evaluation implementation at <https://github.com/GothenburgBitFactory/taskwarrior/blob/v2.5.3/src/Eval.cpp>.

Without thinking much about it, I assumed we would just be parsing expressions like `+home or +phone`. Or `(+a or +b) and +c`.

But reviewing the above link, I realize it's a bit more complicated.

And reviewing `task show filter` reminds me we'd start adding other filters to parse, like `status:pending`, etc.

The above is just to suggest that the problem might be a bit complicated, and perhaps proves that parsing the Taskwarrior syntax should perhaps not block adding other alternative conveniences to Foreground, for example being able to filter on a union of constraints, without the use of Taskwarrior's syntax for report and context filters.

On the other hand, I had an idea on a library to use once and if work on a parser begins. The PetitParser library might be a good fit, at <https://github.com/petitparser/java-petitparser>.

And, in the title, I suggest covering just a subset of the expression syntax and grammar, but I'm not actually sure if that is practical. Maybe someone can help with ideas about how it could be reasonable to cover only a subset of the grammar, to make the initial work more tractable.

Note, if you hard-code the default reports, you can get around this issue until parsing is added.

Title: Feature request: hide checked items in shopping list
Body: I typically have a long shopping list in multiple categories. I sometimes shop online so don't do one category at a time. I'd like to be able to toggle between showing and hiding checked items so that I only see the items that are unchecked even if _some, but not all_ items are checked in a category. I like the categories so I don't want everything in a single category (a workaround that might work for some). I have already activated to "Collapse checked categories" but it's not enough.

Title: Notes Enhancements
Body:
First of all, great app I love using it. Everything is great. Just few tips on the notes part I would like. First would be addition of checkboxes, bullet points for lists and ability to add attachments, like photos or documents or pdfs.


Another addition I would like would be a separate trash bar for notes especially. So if a note is deleted by mistake or is useful for some reason in future it can be restored. Current method of doing it is a kinda jarring. Also an option to clear the trash automatically within 30 days or set a period to do clear it automatically. 


If there is a confusion with my suggestions, please look at google keep, I would prefer an interface for notes similar to that as I find that interface just very easy to jot down something fast when needed.


Again, thank you for creating such a beautiful app.

Title: Request: Add Markdown formatting to Notes
Body:
Please add Markdown formatting to Notes.

In my experience, the best way to implement this is to simply display the formatting in place. In other words, text *within asterisks* should show the asterisks, but be displayed in italics.

Body:
I've been using this app for quite some time and wanted to automatically back-up my notes using DavX and a Nextcloud server.
Unfortunately i discoverd the notes were not saved as plain files, but had to be manually exported before being tampered with by other apps.

A simple question: why?
What makes keeping these files inside the app better than saving them as plain files to begin with ?

I'd hate to see my notes being lost because i didn't back them up manually for a week, while the rest of my contacts, mails, photos etc… are automatically handled by my backup setup. Keeping the notes locked into the app behind an import/export system seems strange and counter-intuitive to me.

Title: Problem: Accidentally deleted files
Body:
I like Notepad very much as it is simple, elegant yet effective. 

The only big problem I have is, that I have over time accidentally deleted several notes.

To avoid this is I suggest these changes

1) The Edit/Save-Button right next to the Delete-Button is screaming for trouble! It is only a question of time till one presses the wrong button.

Solutions:
 a) move the Delete-button as far away as possible from the Edit/Save-Button

 b) or move the Delete-Button into the ... menu


2) I use "Ask before save" in case I accidentally scramble my note. Therefore I always have a dialogue-box before saving.

The problem is, if I accidentally pressed delete instead of save I also get a dialogue-box (that should warn me).
But as I expect the normal save-box (and do not really read attentively each time the text in the box) I will end up deleting the note.

Solutions:
 a) Change the colour of the Delete-message-box for example to red. Then one can clearly distinguish it (at a glance) from the Save-message-box.

 b) Do not delete the note but add the prefix [TRASH] to its  name, as a simple recycle bin. 
If the user then deletes a note with this prefix it will be really deleted.


3)  I use "Ask before save" in case I accidentally scramble my note. 
But most of the time I do not need to discard my changes but want to save.
So the user has to do most of the times unnecessary work.

Solution:
 a) Instead of "Ask before save" add a command "Do not save the changes" to the ... menu.
That way, in the rare case the note has been accidentally scrambled, one can choose this option.
In all the normal cases (overwhelming majority), the note will be saved without having to confirm anything (faster).

I wish you a good health &
thank you very much for your great work!

Title: App closed and Note was not saved in draft
Body:
During a frantic and clumsy attempt to select text from the Notepad using my Chromebook, and with Control+C...., the Notepad app closed and the Note wasn't saved as draft when I re-opened the app.

I think I might have selected some combination of keys causing the app to close like that.
What are the shortcuts for closing the app without the Note being saved in drafts, (if such a shortcut even exists)?

I guess I might have closed the Note, which I did not save, and closed the app in quick succession as a result of my clumsy keyboarding.
This has only happened once.

If I run the app, and close it using task manager, the Note is in draft, but not Saved in the list of saved Notes. To actually save the Note, I need to click the floppy disk save icon.

I guess there must be a shortcut that will close the active Note without saving and deleting the temporary draft, and close the app?

OR

The app crashed, deleting the temporary draft, and closing the App?

**UPDATE:**

1 - I copied text (plain text with UL list without styling, the styling CSS was removed during pasting into Note, which was copied from a website) and pasted into a new Note
2 - I copied one item of text from Note and minimised Notepad App to view Chrome browser window, pasted text into text field on website
3 - Re-focused window (clicked on tray to maximise, Chromebook)...
3.1 - Notepad crashed, any Draft notes deleted automatically.

Was it still my clumsy control+c, control+v keyboarding? Not sure. I don't believe it was.

I have about 50 saved notes, but just small bits of texts, that's all.

Basically, when I re-focus the app to copy the next snip of text, it closes and any drafts are deleted. That's what I found.

**UPDATE 2**

I have managed to repeat the issue:

1 - open App (Chromebook)
2 - copy arbitrary text (mouse pad select + control+c) into new Note without saving Note
3 - focus Google Chrome window via the Chromebook app tray
4 - paste text into somewhere else, anywhere
5 - go back to Notepad by selecting the app icon from the tray, copy more text (mouse pad + control C), refocus Chrome window, paste
6 - go back to Notepad, and with the same text selected, I use shift+left arrow key, to fine select text from the same line
7 - Notepad closes, I re-open Notepad, drafts deleted

Title: [BUG] Cannot disable forced random Passphrase/Password Question when Biometric Lock is enabled
Body:
I'm using this app regularly, because it's mostly useful.

However, there is one thing about it, that seriously annoys the absolute crap out of me.
Whenever I open the app, especially when I need to really quickly note something ASAP, I get this pop-up asking, whether I remember my passphrase and I am forced to put it in, even though I have biometric locking enabled.

Like, just as any other sane person, my password is saved in a password manager, so I don't need this app blocking my work flow, especially when I need to note something as quickly as possible.

The worst & most taunting about this is, that it happens seemingly randomly. I cannot expect it or anything. It just happens sometimes.
I don't know how this app does it, but it always appears, whenever I need to open the app really quickly.

This is so infuriating, that I really consider this a huge bug. This is not a feature request, it is a request for a big face biting bug to be fixed.

## Suggestions
One or more of the following could be a valid solution.

* Add an Enable/Disable Toggle Button.
* Let the user decide how often or when the passphrase is asked for.
* Let the user manually enable "Ask me next time" so the user knows, it will ask only the next time the app is opened.

Title: Profiles/Accounts
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
To have multiple users/profiles inside Safenotes

**Describe the solution you'd like**
To go to the app menu and choose which profile to use. The ability to have multiple profiles, such as one profile for work notes, one profile for personal notes.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
None

**Additional context**

- The option to have an optional master password for the entire app (All profiles)
- The option to have a password to access each profile
- Each profile will encrypt their notes with a different password/key
- There are settings for each profile and there is preferences which are settings for the entire app.

Title: Choose A Folder/Directory To Store Notes
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
A directory to store and sync encrypted notes in

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The ability to choose to have notes stored inside the app or choose a directory to store the notes on your device.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Similar to how Joplin can encrypt notes that are stored in a directory. but Joplin does not have a native mobile app making notes stored in a folder and using syncthing unstable and very buggy.

**Additional context**
Therefore uses can use Syncthing to sync notes between devices such as their phone and tablet. Notes metadata and note file names should also be encrypted.

App will have to sync with local directory every time it launches and as it update notes.

Title: Partial product check
Body:
Idea for an additional feature: 

Let's say you wanted to purchase 6 packs of rice from that particular brand in the store ABC.
So you go to that store, but they have only 3 packs left. You purchase them but you need to remember you should still find 3 other packs somewhere else (or come back later to the same store).

In the app, so far you can either:
- check the product (qty=6) and reset the field with a quantity of 3 (but if then you check the 3, it will mess up your stats as it will count 9 when you only purchased 6)
- edit the product and change quantity to 3 (but if you check that later, your stats will have not taken into account the 3 packs you had already purchased before).
  
It would be great to have the possibility check only part the quantity required. For instance:
- one tap on the product card = checks the product (as in current behaviour)
- two quick taps on the product card = open a dialog box prompting you to enter the quantity you already purchased. Then it would make the difference with the total quantity required, pass this info to the statistics, and would display only the remaining quantity required.

Title: Refactor Member Ignoring Method smell in ShoppingListConverterImpl class
Body:
Hi, I'm Emanuele Iannone, a master student at University of Salerno.
Since my bachelor's thesis I have been working on a **code smell refactoring plugin** called *aDoctor*, which is able to identify and fix energy-related problems in Android apps.
I launched it on your project, finding different instances of code smells. I chose one of them and let the plugin automatically fix it.
In this case I chose Member Ignoring Method, that is present when a non static method does not use at all instance variables and other non static methods. These kind of smell may have a non trivial impact on energy consumption, as shown in this paper: `https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0950584918301678`.
Besides, this kind of refactoring does not impact on the functionalities of your app, so it is totally safe. Let me know if you are interested in this refactoring proposal.

Title: Suggestions by @jaswinder77
Body:
[Reference](https://t.me/smartpack_kmanager/29888)

Some suggestions:

> Support regular font too in addition to existing bold font.

> Inter-convertible notes & checklists as we see in these apps:

Notes
https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.gcteam.tonote

MaxNote
https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.maxciv.maxnote

> Clearing notes should not be so easy. Having such an option could be helpful for you as a developer or to beta testers but it should not be easily accessible to a regular user, or should not be available at all for them (as they always have the option of uninstalling to remove all the notes). 
Or maybe, if it has to exist, it could be available in a "Advanced..." section at the bottom. There can be a dropdown arrow to the right of it. Clicking it could show the option to clear all notes.

 It should at least ask you to type some text like "Delete all my notes" (case sensitive) or it should ask for password or fingerprint. Whatever be the check, the user should be made to realize that it is something serious. It should also not be too easy for a child who has got  hold of the phone to clear the notes accidentally.

For this, if possible, the UI should be such that default probability or likelihood to cancel the command should be deliberately made much higher than the probability of hitting delete.

> lists can be improved a lot. Pressing enter should take cursor to next item. There can be alphabetical sorting and sorting by status (move checked items to the bottom).

> Cursor is not visible on default background color for notes, at least on my device.

Body: This is a feature request for sub-tasks in Privacy Friendly Todo List. It would be very useful if they could be re-ordered. For example, if some sub-task pops into my mind later but already added sub-tasks depend on it, it would give me more overall clarity to move that sub-tasks up.

Title: Missing info about how-to back up this app's data
Body: Missing info about how-to back up this app's data to make it clear to users how they can do this. This is very necessary when a user is switching physical phones.

Title: reminder time ignores timezone
Body:
Every time I set up an alarm for a note it uses the time I specify and converts it to another timezone (CET?)

For example, if I set the alarm for a specific local time and then I edit it, it is many hours in the future.

Title: Show note creation date.
Body: There is currently no way to see the date and time that a note had been created. This information should be shown in the note, and possibly an option to show it on the list screen as well.

Title: Audio note: Save button does not save
Body:
When recording an Audio note, pressing the Save button does not actually save the note and return to the list screen. Rather, it discards the note and returns to the list screen, same as the Back arrow button.

Pressing the Save button should save the note and return to the list screen, even if it is in the middle of recording.

Title: Reduce friction of adding a new note.
Body:
A few changes would greatly reduce the friction of adding a new note:

1. Instead of a single "+" button that then pops up a selection of new note types, have four separate buttons - one for each note type.
2. In the Checklist and Text note types, have the text field already focused. This will allow the user to start typing automatically.
3. In the Audio note type, start the recording immediately instead of waiting for the user to press the record button.

Title: [Feature request] Add different calendar views
Body:
I have been having thoughts about moving my tasks to a calendar app.  The advantage is that I have a week view and can see in one screen a schedule of the upcoming tasks and the time which those tasks need to started/ended.  To allow this, ALL tasks must have a date and time whether it be a Start/End Date or a Reminder Date/Time.  However, I like some of my tasks to have no Date/Time.

Currently, the Calendar view only shows the current month with no "summary" of what the tasks are within each day of the month.

Calendar apps (including Microsoft Outlook) generally have the following views:

- A view of the day and the hours within the day
- 7 days display on the screen with the hours within each day
- A month calendar with a compact display of each task within each day

I don't expect the different calendar views to be exactly like calendar apps because SecUSo ToDo is not a a calendar app.

This Feature Request is obviously not easy, but I log this so that it is in the records.

Backup & restore from the local storage is also sufficient for a great starting point and in future some cloud storage can be considered optionally if required. Please consider.

Title: 'isunlock' may always be false when 'onSaveInstanceState' in 'MainActivity'
Body:
I notice that in 'MainActivity', both 'onStop' and 'onSaveInstanceState' use the field 'isunlock'. However, accoding to the Android official documents, the execution order of these two lifecycle callbacks may reverse after Android API 28. 
In this case, if users use devices at or over  API 28, 'isunlock' is always set to false in 'onStop' and afterwards saved into the bundle in 'onSaveInstanceState'.

Title: Activity memory leak caused by anonymous threads
Body:
# Possible Memory Leak
An [anonymous inner class](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/17778077/would-anonymous-handler-or-runnable-create-a-memory-leak) will hold a reference to the this pointer of the outer class and will not be released until the thread ends.
It will hold the Activity and prevent its timely release. Please check the links below.

# Occurrences
https://github.com/SubhamTyagi/android-ocr/blob/master/app/src/main/java/io/github/subhamtyagi/ocr/MainActivity.java#L314

# Possible Solution
If it is necessary, it can be changed to [static class + weak reference](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/22162642/will-handler-inside-a-method-leak-memory/27825703#27825703) to eliminate the reference to the activity, which may cause memory leaks.
Further discussion is welcome.

Title: German recognition of word breaks at the end of lines not treated correctly when concatenating lines
Body:
In German word breaks at the end of some line are represented by a "-" directly after the last character of the word.

The Android App "OCR" concatenates lines appending a space after the line-break character.
Otherwise OCR does a superb job!

The issue could easily be fixed with the following procedure when concatenating lines:

If a line contains a "-" at the end, check the first word of the next line:
> if the word is "und" or "oder" keep the "-" and insert a space when concatenating the lines (current procedure)
> with other first word: when the word starts with a lower case character drop the "-" when concatenating the lines, otherwise keep the "-"; do NOT insert an additional space when concatenating the lines.



![TesseractOcrAndroid-TestPage](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/79666263/121798933-7d216c80-cc29-11eb-81b2-0c2f78da364d.jpeg)
![2021-06-13 08_Tesseract-German-Recognition](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/79666263/121798934-7e529980-cc29-11eb-9d55-a936fca26776.png)

Title: [Feature request] Allow option to use external secondary storage (sd-card etc)
Body:
### Describe what you'd like to see added to the app

Currently, Anemo uses internal private storage. 
This is very useful to keep files private from mediastore and other apps with storage access peeking around. 

One limitation of this choice is that it isn't taking advantage of expandable storage (like SD-cards). Internal storage is limited, and can fill up easily. 

With Android 11, app specific directories on external storage are also private to the app. Inaccessible to other apps, including those with general write access.
[[ref: android-11-privacy]] 

[ref: android-11-privacy]: https://developer.android.com/about/versions/11/privacy/storage#other-app-specific-dirs

It would be useful to have an option in Anemo to use this external storage. 
Either multiple Anemo providers, or option to select between internal memory and secondary storage.

Title: [Problem] Remote wiki isn't cached offline
Body:
Hi, I found this app while looking for a better way to use TiddlyWiki on my Android.  
I don't know if this is a bug or just a missing feature, but the remote (import from an URL) wiki feature doesn't work properly.

I added my wiki from GitLab Pages, and while my device is connected to the Internet I can use it fine. If I close the wiki (get back to the main menu) but the app remains in RAM, I can even reopen the wiki without an Internet connection.  
However, if the app gets closed, then the wiki will refuse to open again if I don't have Internet: I get the Android webview error screen saying the server is unreachable (and a Tiddloid popup saying the wiki doesn't exist anymore online, and asking me if I want to remove it).

Tiddloid should cache the HTML files of the remote wiki in the device storage for offline availability. On open, it should check if the server is reachable, and if so, it downloads the file from that. If the server isn't reachable, then the latest cached HTML file gets loaded instead, and all the saves get saved locally.  
TiddlyWiki will of course be unable to sync changes remotely in this state, so Tiddloid should set a flag to make it possible for the wiki to sync the next time there is Internet; for example, the very next time there is Internet, it's still the local HTML file that gets loaded, so that one has a chance of letting TiddlyWiki sync changes. Exposing a button in the app's status bar to switch between latest cached version and online version would also work fine, if the all-automatic solution is too unreliable.

For some extra context: I have my wiki configured to sync changes to GitLab, and this works fine even in Tiddloid. My device is a tablet running armv7 Android 8.1.0 (Samsung Experience 9.5). I run the latest APK of Tiddloid downloaded from the Releases of this repo.

Thanks for your time!

But currently tiddloid restricts the "supported" file extensions to `.html` on both WebDAV and SAF, making it impossible to work with that setup.

When a notebook is mostly used as an inbox, e. g. for new tasks or information stored on the go, it makes sense to archive tasks / info that have become irrelevant to a designated 'archive' or 'bin' notebook (always the same one) rather than deleting them straight away (to allow recovery in case I change my mind and do need to retrieve some info). It would be very useful to have a dedicated 'archive' or 'move to bin' button on one of the popup toolbars for this. As it is, I have to tap on refile and then select the target notebook each time. When > 95% of my refiling is to the same 'archive' notebook anyway, this is not very efficient.

Thanks!

Title: No 'cut' options on popup when selecting text in the 'Title' edit control

To reproduce: tap on an already exisiting / titled note to start editing it. If you tap on the text in the 'Title' edit box, the keyboard will be shown and now the popup on selecting text will include a 'Cut' option.

However, if you start by long-pressing on the text in the 'Title' edit box to select a word, the keyboard is not shown and there is no 'cut' option in the text selection popup. So you can now neither cut nor delete the selected text. You have to tap and thus lose the selection to get bring up the keyboard, then select the text again to have the option.

Thanks!

Title: Support for `org-log-states-order-reversed nil`
Body:
As documented in the org-mode source code:
```elisp
(defcustom org-log-states-order-reversed t
  "Non-nil means the latest state note will be directly after heading.
When nil, the state change notes will be ordered according to time.

This option can also be set with on a per-file-basis with

   #+STARTUP: logstatesreversed
   #+STARTUP: nologstatesreversed"
  :group 'org-todo
  :group 'org-progress
  :type 'boolean)
```

It would be nice to have an equivalent, app-wide, setting in Orgzly.

Title: Formatting of description in Fastlane
Body:
If you saw how your `full_description.txt` renders at F-Droid, maybe you're interested in some improved formatting? You can find [some formatting hints here](https://gitlab.com/IzzyOnDroid/repo/-/wikis/Fastlane). Most importantly, you should avoid using "headings" (`

` & co) – and be aware that F-Droid converts each line break into `
\n`. Giving you an idea of how to achive better rendering: ```markdown Fujiten is a Japanese dictionary application made with the flutter framework. Definition and kanji comes from the EDICT dictionary, compiled as a database from the edict_database project.
Setup In order to fujiten to work, the Expression Database and the Kanji Database are needed. The databases can be downloaded and installed from fujiten via the "settings/databases menu or by downloading the database manually from https://github.com/odrevet/edict_database.
Top menu Bars Access the settings menu. … ``` This should work well with F-Droid – and also with Play and with my repo (where your app was listed until now; I usually remove apps from my repo after a decent "overlap", i.e. 10..14 days after they reached F-Droid. Should you want me to keep your app, please let me know).

Body:

Title: Importing a category from file can generate duplicate categories
Body:
Found a bug that when importing a category from a file like JSON.
If the imported category has the same name as a category already on your system it will generate another category with the same name.
This can cause problems down the line and is generally very confusing.

We should check if a category exists with the same name before importing. And if it does we should update the new category by appending a incremental number after the name. 
For example:
- `General` category exists
- Go to import a category named `General` from file
- The new category created should be named `General_01`

This functionality exists when creating a category [here](https://github.com/vukani-dev/improvement-roll/blob/2ca215d7c88a31f109490c3a3cea12cb5f6d46a5/screens/AddCategory.js#L156)

Just need to do something similar when [importing a category](https://github.com/vukani-dev/improvement-roll/blob/2ca215d7c88a31f109490c3a3cea12cb5f6d46a5/screens/ImportExport.js#L105).

Title: Feature request: Enabling sequences of tasks with "id:" and "after:"
Body:
First, thanks a lot for this great app. I installed it a while ago, but just recently discovered its life-changing potential for myself.
I use Simpletask in conjunction with [pter](https://codeberg.org/vonshednob/pter) on my Linux machine. In addition to the actual todo.txt-syntax, pter supports sequences of tasks with the key:value pairs "id:xyz" and "after:xyz". With these, you can give a unique ID to one task and then refer to it in another task, making the second one show up only after the first has been completed. Example:
`Wash laundry due:2023-05-12 id:1`
`Dry laundry after:1`
If Simpletask could understand this syntax as well, this would make handling recurring sequences of small tasks a breeze. Also, planning bigger projects beforehand would become much easier. A big part of the weekly review could be automated.

Added permission check/get/test on change of setting.

Could be extended to choose filter etc in settings.

Tested in emulator (API 30) and on my Samsung S22 (Android 13) for a few days.

Title: Android 13: Simpletask Cloudness: Can't select the folder
Body:
Good evening

Thank you for the app. After giving the app the permissions, I cannot select the folder for the todo.txt file. 

Do I need to adjust anything else under Settings?

I want to synchronise the todo.txt file via Syncthing. I can already sync my Markdown files between Markor and Zettlr.

Title: Problem with WebDAV version
Body:
Dear all,

I have now twice experienced the following problem with Simpletask on an Android 11 device.

I open the app and after a few seconds the app attempts to synchronize. It comes back with "Unable to synchronize Todo.txt".

This result remains the case, even though internet access is normal; even though I stopped & restarted the app and even after rebooting the phone.

I then tried to just reload the todo.txt from the nextcloud-server, but since I had modified the local file on the device, the app won't let me.

I then thought I would log out of the Webdav account and log in again, but unfortunately the app does not retain the Webdav credentials; whenI logged out they were gone.

I have now switched back to synching with Dropbox, where this has (up to now) never happened; (but that means I need a Dropbox client for Sleek on my desktop machines which I do not really want) any idea what the problem could be here?

Kind regards,
Oliver.

Title: Fix bug where notifications can disappear/lose association with task
Body:
Since adding the ability to pin tasks as notifications in #1170 I've found a couple issues with the current implementation. Namely:
- Sometimes pinned notifications will randomly disappear. It seems like this was caused by the app being restarted by the OS/crashing in the background as I could reproduce in an emulator by running `adb shell am force-stop nl.mpcjanssen.simpletask.debug`.

Solution: Create the notifications from a `Service` and call `startForeground`.

- If the todo list is modified outside of simpletask, the task IDs will change causing the notifications to lose their association with each task.

Solution: Make the task IDs persistent by storing them in `SharedPreferences`.

Title: fix for losing data during rotation of device
Body:
Hello again Knirirr

I was using the BeeCount app and I noticed another activity that can lead to data loss. When the user goes to edit the count settings for an item as can be seen here: https://ibb.co/N2LHbNJ

If the screen focus goes to another app or activity(for example if an incoming call forces the user to go into the phone app), or if the user rotates the phone and changes the app to landscape mode, the user will lose any data they had put into this page as can be seen here: https://ibb.co/C09QQCf

I have used savedInstanceState to automatically save the data in such cases and restore the data when the user returns to the BeeCount app(and hence the activity) or after the phone is rotated. Therefore, the user does not have to fill in the data again thus improving the user experience.

I have tested out the feature to ensure it works.

If you have any questions or if you would like me to change anything, please do not hesitate to let me know!

Thank you for your time,
Tim

Title: Option for Mic Input button?
Body:
So something I see a lot with FOSS keyboards is they typically have no dedicated voice input button, meaning you need to go down to the keyboard selector and choose the voice input keyboard to use it.

If there was an option to have a little microphone button above the keyboard (even if it means doing away with the predictive text, optionally of course) it would be a lot more convenient.

FlorisBoard has a good way of showcasing this, as it works well. I like all other aspects of this keyboard better though, i just really wish it had that one button. I've attached a screenshot showcasing the function buttons above the number row on FlorisBoard as an example.
![Screenshot_20231123-223602_1](https://github.com/openboard-team/openboard/assets/99198915/bc215e19-69a7-4704-b114-7dc20e989035)

As an edit since I glossed over this: i'm aware that the button I seek dynamically pops up when it is detected that it can be used, but I wish there was a toggle in settings to have it always be displayed no matter what, as the clipboard button is. ;)

Title: option to disable all auto-capitalization
Body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
i almost never want anything i type that isn't formal to be "properly" capitalised. this also seems to be a common sentiment with younger people in general in the circles im in -- having "correct" caps comes off as "trying too hard".

choosing what case you use has a significant impact on the tone and personality of your message, and is becoming an intentional choice more than ever before, so it would be nice if openboard trusted your intent with it.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
an option to disable all suggestions and replacements that adjust case -- including proper nouns, abbreviations, and "i" (first person pronoun). if correction is needed, it should correct spelling but preserve the case the word was written in -- whether that's all caps, all lower, or "correct" sentence-casing (stretch: "mOcKiNg CaSe" too?)

the current options only allow disabling uppercase on the first character after a full stop.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

hitting backspace on every suggestion that auto capitalises, manually changing the text

**Additional context**

maybe related to #681?

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fS4X1JfX6_Q

tl;dr: case has little meaning other than what we agree it does, and for many people, playing with casing is an intentional choice.

Title: Better support for Galaxy Fold
Body:
Editing per great suggesrion by @StormyIceLeopard.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Tl;Dr; OpenBoard's keyboard layouts / sizing and ability to adapt between open (double width) and closed/cover (narrow) views make it difficult and frequently outright unusable on the Fold

Details: The keyboard is much too wide for gesture typing when the phone is opened up. It's also so tall that it takes up a disproportionate amount of screen real estate to that its hard to see the screen's content. Scaling down the height helps, but a reasonable height (eg 50-60%) for the opened phone is now disproportionately too wide, and then becomes much too small to use when the phone is closed (on the narrow cover screen). Similarly the "one hand" mode it's still much too wide to easily gesture type with your thumb. Finally, and I hesitate to write this as I realize it is quite subjective, but personally I find the Split Keyboard layout to be unusable (too difficult/unnatural to use) on the Open Fold and so not a viable option. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Implement a split keyboard using the key layout / spacing / sizing of GBoard's split keyboard, and have it then switch to a normal/appropriately sized keyboard when the phone is closed. This seems to be the layout that works best (by a very long shot to anything I've found) on the Fold.

And then, provide a thumb option for gesture typing that provides the correct proportions when the Fold is open (and returns to the normal keyboard when closed). Not to be a total GBoard fanboy/girl, but I would again recommend using GBoard's sizing/spacing for this thumb keyboard as it feels quite natural. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Ability to scale both the width and the height for all of the different options (normal, split, thumb) with different settings for when phone is open (or in landscape) vs when is closed. This strikes me as quite problematic/much less flexible to new form factors and sizes that might appear. So I don't think this is a good option.

Title: toki pona language
Body:
(sorry for not doing this via a PR, I'm just not that good at git)
[toki pona](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toki_Pona) is a 21st century minimalist constructed language. it can be written with the Latin character set, and has a very small vocabulary - the dictionary below has 257, with many being neologisms (nimi sin) that are not widely used (don't worry, the frequencies are adjusted down accordingly).
the vocabulary is taken from [ilo Linku](https://linku.la), a database of all toki pona words in use that is updated yearly based on a community-wide survey. ilo Linku data is licensed under [CC by-sa 3.0 and 4.0 combined license](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creative_Commons_license) ([proof 1](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xwgTAxwgn4ZAc4DBnHte0cqta1aaxe112Wh1rv9w5Yk/edit#gid=1610334520), [proof 2](https://github.com/lipu-linku/jasima)) so no problem to implement it.

in the zipped folder are the .dict file and the .combined.gz file as created by me.

[toki pona.zip](https://github.com/openboard-team/openboard/files/12361800/toki.pona.zip)

Title: What's the mean of Archive?For what use?
Multiple Suggestions (features)
Body:
Hi,

These are not issues, but rather a list of features I would love to see implemented in future releases. There is no suggestions sections, so I do apologise for adding this to the issues section.

1. Implement fonts resizing (larger).
2. Improved search capability, where (a) the number of found matches is indicated, (b) found matches are highlighted in text, and (c) they are scrollable across all matching notes.
3. Automatic bullet & numbering lists (apply bullet formatting or numbering to all selected text, as well as when jumping to a new row - similar to Enter on keyboard).
4. Option in settings to no longer jump to the end of note when editing, but rather keep the cursor at current indexing location (very annoying in large notes to have to come back and search AGAIN, for location intended to be edited).
5. Allow notebooks to be manually (alphabetical) re-sorted in left menu as well as in settings.
6. Implement selective encryption, or at least password / biometric protection to allow for each note to be protected independently.
7. Add a condensed quick view mode to allow as many notes as possible to be viewed at a glance.
8. Add option in settings to start Quillnote either in (1) All Notebooks - overview showing all created notebooks, (2) Specific Notebook, or (3) All notes (current view).

The app has phenomenal potential. I absolutely love it. In fact, it only took me two days to transition from Zoho Notebook to Quillnote.

Thanks!

Title: Activity Acronyms/Shortname
Body: For those of us who add a lot of activities, can we display a shortname for the activities, instead?  We need to be able to have a regular name for usage everywhere else and the ability to enable just shortnames in the selection area.  ie: "Killing a Body And Hiding It In a Bush", could be shortened to, "KaBAHITAB".  This makes it easier to look at a lot of activities at once and make a selection quicker.

Title: Automatic Backups
Body: Could we get automatic backups, set at a custom interval?  I lost three months of backups because my phone bootlooped and I had to format it.  I couldn't gain access to the system directory to grab the raw file, and it was very frustrating to lose that much data all at once.

Title: Add Timestamp To Backup Function
Body: When you click the "Export" button, could we get a timestamp automatically added to the filename?  It's nicer to get an incremental backup than to just keep getting the same filename.  Besides, in case file timestamps change, it's best to have the updates' timestamp in the filename.

Title: A terminated activity could be unexpectedly restarted
Body:
- I have searched the history bug list, and have not found a similar bug.

- The bug was found on the latest version 1.4.0 (and the master branch). The bug can be reproduced on an Android emulator 6.0 and a Google Pixel 3 XL phone.

- Bug description

A terminated activity could be unexpectedly restarted when it is followed by a undo action on any other activity.

- Video of correct functionality

Start an activity ``Cinema`` and terminate it. On the history page, we can see ``Cinema`` was correctly recorded and its state is terminated (with specific start time and end time)

![20200227_112750](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3156978/75446156-2d049500-5967-11ea-8842-5067d210183a.gif)


- Video of incorrect functionality

Start an activity ``Cinema`` and terminate it. Then, we start ``Sleeping`` and undo it. According to my understanding, the app now should show ``<No Activity>`` on top.  However, it shows ``Cinema``, which was already terminated. On the history page, we can also see ``Cinema`` was unexpectedly restarted (only w/ start time but wo/ end time).

![20200227_112827](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3156978/75446163-2fff8580-5967-11ea-8509-03f7ff51d979.gif)

Title: Incorrectly show/allow a pic when no activity is active/started
Body:
- I have searched the history bug list, and have not found a similar bug.

- The bug was found on the latest version 1.4.0 (also on the master branch). The bug can be reproduced on an Android emulator 6.0 and a Google Pixel 3 XL phone.

- Bug description

 When no activity is active (i.e., ``<No Activity>`` is shown on the top), if the user tries to take a picture, he/she cannot load the picture. This is a correct functionality because no activity is started. In other words, it makes senses to disallow or avoid showing a picture to be taken for an "\<No Activity\>".

 However, if the user selects an activity, e.g., ``Cinema`` and directly undo it. Then, he/she can successfully load a picture for ``<No Activity>``. This is an obviously incorrect/inconsistent functionality.

Title: "Terminate Activity By Click" Setting Doesn't Properly Disable
Body:
I have noticed that when you turn off the setting to have activities terminate by clicking them again, it still allows you to terminate them and replace the activity field with the default, `<No Activity Selected>`.

- Waiting for the activities to change can be slow and the app often freezes, making it appear that the new activity wasn't truly selected.  Clicking it again will finally select the activity, unfreezing the app but also registering as having clicked it twice, essentially selecting the new activity and simultaneously un-selecting it, leaving it as `<No Activity Selected>`, which defies the settings in the app's menu.  This also leads to the creation of sloppy entries in the log, with overlapping timestamps, causing strange things to happen with the "Adjacent Entry" algorithms, if you will. 
- Whenever there is an activity selected, it also still shows in the list of activities that can be selected.  Selecting that same activity will cancel itself out, leaving, `<No Activity Selected>`.

Title: Switching Activities Often Leaves Sloppy Duplicates and Incorrect Activity Selection
Body:
![Screenshot_20191127-Activity_Diary(c)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11280085/69701194-bf5e8800-112f-11ea-9f80-23f79846fa19.png)

I am using a dumby activity to show you this bug.

1. My database is chock-full of occurrences much like this, in which the activities don't align with timestamps correctly, and it's often multiple activities started at once and ending in different times.
2. These are all different activities being selected, yet they all ended up as the same one.

Notice there are overlapping end times.  There are many instances in my database in which there are two or three starting in the same time, as well.

This is really frustrating when trying to track different project phases and they all end up being mislabeled without a way to edit those activity numbers from within the app itself.

Possible ways to recreate:

- Have over 5k activities in the log, causing massive lag and exponentially long load times
- Minimizing the app without waiting a full minute or two
- Closing the app without first waiting for over a minute
- Changing activities without first leaving the app open for over a minute
- Sometimes it happens anyway, even if waiting for 1~10mins with the app actively open

I have had this problem for over a year.  I thought maybe once I had switched to a super high performance phone, it would compensate for the inefficient algorithms in the app.  However, even after I had gone from having a nice phone to having a _really_ nice phone (12GB RAM / Snapdragon 855 / Large storage / etc.), it still behaves very slowly and has a plethora of bugs caused by its internal algorithms which can't be switched off.

It would appear to me that instead of simply inserting a new activity into the log, which you would expect from a seemingly simple app, it also changes the most recent entry to be whatever is still stuck as the currently-selected tile.  Meaning, the algorithm takes so long to switch to whatever activity is selected as next that it updates the activities to be whatever was previously selected.

If none of that makes sense, I will try to explain in more detail.

Basically, I can no longer import my database back into ActivityDiary.  I've been trying all different methods, all day. Title: Add options for two types of vibration
Body:
I found this program in F-Droid and I really liked it!
But she has some nuances of use.

ABOUT VIBRATIONS (in the main menu and a five-minute vibration)

Vibration of the main menu
 This vibration occurs every time you enter the main window (which is undoubtedly correct, but each return to the main window is again accompanied by vibration and it is times annoyed)
Please make this option:
 Main window vibration - on/off

Vibration every five minutes
 This vibration is not very annoying, except during video viewing (at such moments, a reminder of vibration, it just wastes energy and brings repair of the vibrating mechanism of a smartphone closer)
 Here would be the option of minute intervals?  (0 - disabled and 5/10/15/20/25/30 - minute intervals of such vibration)

Perhaps this is a trifle, but not everyone needs constant (and without settings) vibration.
 I hope someday to see these options in the program.

Title: en dvorak: add ð & þ
Body:
Eth & thorn, ð & þ, were historic letters to English & still common in Icelandic words. There seems to be recurring movements online to use them in English today too to supplant the most common digraph, “th”, for [θ] [ð] sounds.

Placement was at the beginning of the list as these characters have higher codepoint values than the others (altho the order doesn’t have an order I personally understand—and ideally users could reorder them).

Title: Hebrew uses a deprecated language code
Body:
In situations where the language code is displayed (such as the word editor and the next word settings), AnySoftKeyboard uses the old, deprecated-in-1989 language code `iw` rather than the modern `he`.
![Screenshot_20231226-194317_AnySoftKeyboard](https://github.com/AnySoftKeyboard/AnySoftKeyboard/assets/109424320/8561995b-efb8-4351-8022-34fc1a27796c)

Interestingly, Indonesian, which was deprecated the same year, does use the correct code:
![Screenshot_20231226-195140_AnySoftKeyboard](https://github.com/AnySoftKeyboard/AnySoftKeyboard/assets/109424320/c9858319-b959-4412-8501-a11c8365d509)

From [this comment](https://github.com/tuskyapp/Tusky/issues/2903#issuecomment-1323741899) on another project that had that issue, I suspect that whoever made the Indonesian addon used proper [ISO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639) while the maker of the Hebrew addon used Android's locale list and that's it, which is strangely out-of-date.

Title: ANR when typing after selecting link
Body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Install [this notetaking app](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/com.maltaisn.notes.sync/) from F-droid. [This](https://f-droid.org/repo/com.maltaisn.notes.sync_10503.apk) is the apk if you prefer a direct link.
2. Press the `+` button at the bottom right, to make a note.
3. Type `example.org   hello` as the note content (below the title).
4. Press ASK's left arrow. This selects the link.
   4.1. The fact that the link is selected is reported [here](https://github.com/maltaisn/another-notes-app/issues/127), but I think that ASK should not give an ANR in any case.
5. While the link is selected,
5.1. if you type `(` then ASK freezes and there is the ANR `Reason: Input dispatching timed out (Waiting to send non-key event because the touched window has not finished processing certain input events that were delivered to it over 500.0ms ago.  Wait queue length: 14.  Wait queue head age: 79937.5ms.)`
5.2. if you have typed a letter instead of `(`, it has replaced the link text, as expected.
7. To restart ASK, I open another app, focus a text box, wait a few seconds.

This happens with Italian and German language packs on ASK 1.11.7137 + Samsung Android 11, Samsung Android 7.1.

(edited for clarity)

Title: Feature-include impending closure of bracket/quote
Body:
It is tedious to have to do twice (so I manually do this), please allow **auto closure** of brackets, quotes, tags and other paired symbols.
• When user presses ( or ‘or “ or [ or { or < or « key,
• Place ) or ” or ’ or ] or } or > or » ahead of cursor (allow math people disable < action).
• When a second space is pressed the cursor moves to outside of closure object (maybe one for tags).

- Some people use nested brackets and quotes, permit piling them up in closure sequence beyond cursor. possessive (both’ place’s) and abbreviation apostrophes are closing types and will not interfere if system responds only in the defined ways (’til and ’90s). I like to put commas after brackets (as here), so keyboard's existing comma/period move function needs to be applied too.

Example: Cursor shall be |
• Trigger symbol added, next placed ahead.
He said "|"
• Continue typing
He said "Quickly|"
• One space still continues
He said "Quickly! |"
• Second space closes (any proceeding space temporarily absorbed, maybe temporarily shuttle any proceeding punctuation too)
He said "Quickly!" |
• Comma shuttled across when pressed (subsequent space press could be ignored)
He said "Quickly!", |
N.B. keyboard theme hinders "/ “ / ” recognition, left mistake present.

Title: [feature request] Non-Breaking Space
Body:
Hi devs and everybody,

It would be a good feature to be able to make non-breaking spaces with the keyboard.

In french, it is required for big punctuation: before `:`, `;`, `!`, `?`, `»` and after  `«`.
But I think it could be useful for anybody who wants to keep a text in shape. It is usable in several cases:

- for dates:                                                                           November 9th 2023
- before currency unit symbols:                 100 $, 100 €
- before measurement unit symbols: 11 feet, 200 km, 60 kg, 25 kN
- before percent symbol:                                    90 %
- before degree symbol:                                      20 °C, 68 °F
- as a thousands separator:                             1 000 000

Non-breaking spaces allow us that prevents an automatic line break at its position and keeps the text consistent.

Title: FR: Time Aspect
Body: Thanks for Open Pros & Cons at F-Droid! Sometimes it is beneficial to differentiate between short and long term consequences. Could you consider adding fields below for long term pros and cons as an option? Thank you!

Title: Add tests to the drag and drop functionality
Body:
Currently, there are no tests for the drag and drop functionality.

It would be great to have e2e tests and unit tests.

Title: Gather test coverage information from cypress
Body: Currently we are sending the test coverage information created by jest to CodeCov. We also want to gather information about the e2e tests.

Title: Set up Android e2e tests
Body: Right now I can test the application with jest and cypress. However, this is only valid for the web application. I want to make sure the tests pass in an android app as well.

- Updated icon
- Some improvements

Title: Time input dialog is cut off
Body: On smaller screens the time input dialog is cut off.

Title: Add volume control for timer
Body: Right now it is tied to the media volume and is pretty loud.

Title: Routine Editor: show total routine length
Body: In the Routine Editor, display the sum of the duration of all of the routine's exercises.

Title: Show session progress
Body: Add some sort of progress indicator to the Session Fragment to show how far along you are with the practice session. For example, a simple fraction (2/5) possibly combined with a linear progress bar.

Title: Add key info to exercises
Body:
Hello! Fantastic app, I've been searching for something like this for a long time.

I've found I've been creating exercises (such as different arps) repeatedly, one for each scale. It'd be great to be able to create one exercise, then keep track of the BPM information along with what scale was practiced.

Thank you!

Title: Import/Export data
Body:
Add data export/backup options:
- [ ] full database export/import
- [ ] zipped session history as TXT and BPM records as CSV
- [x] [Google Auto Backup](https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/data/autobackup)

Title: Add a metronome
Body:
Having a metronome built into the session would be nice. Using the built in looping functionality of MediaPlayer and SoundPool to play the click has too much latency, but the [NDK](https://developer.android.com/ndk/guides/audio) will probably work.

Edit: the app works great in split screen mode with any other metronome app open, so I don't think this feature will be required.

Title: Allow to manually pick next task, or manual reordering of tasks
Body:
Hello, i think you had an awesome idea with your app, thank you very much for sharing it under open-source!

I would like to suggest to add the possibility to select the current task to do. I often find myself wanting to complete a specific task first, and i cannot mark it as complete before completing other apps. 

An extension of this feature would be the ability to manually reorder all tasks in the list, because the app is currently unforgiving, either you enter the list of tasks in the right order the first time, or you are stuck with a list that doesn't follow the necessary sequence to complete your project.

Title: Make task input bigger
Body:
Currently, the task input is very small (a text field in a dialog) and it's both non-obvious and difficult to enter multiple tasks at once.

This can be solved by creating a fullscreen multiline text field for the input, with a better hint.

Title: [Bug] backspace key doesn't work properly
Body:
**Describe the bug**
When deleting non regular text, 2 backspace key strokes are needed to delete 1 character.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Keyboard
2. Type text with non-regular font
3. Press backspace

**Expected behavior**
Every backspace press removes 1 character

**Android version**
Android 12

**Additional context**
Current behavior is the "special" character turning into a questionmark (? or �) after the first press and then the questionmark getting deleted on the second press.

Title: [Discussion]: Porting, Unifying, Connecting
Body:
**Porting** – to applications or platforms
**Unifying** – styles & conversion
**Connecting** – to other services

🛈 This issue is a discussion about the following topics:

+ Porting the functionality of Irregular Expressions into other applications or platforms
+ Unifying the way text is being converted
+ Unifying text styles
+ Connecting Irregular Expressions to other Services (e.g. style databases)

_The topics of this issue can be changed at any given time (on request)._

⪩ 𝓑𝓮𝓵𝓪 ⪨ ~ NA0341

Title: Global Text Styling
Body:
*This is related to #35*

Reformat selected text anywhere¹ through a Irregular Expressions option in the floating text selection menu.

+ Opt-in feature to be enabled in settings.
+ Tapping on that option creates a floating menu containing buttons with the styles.
+ Decoded input should be cached in order to change the style multiple times.

If possible: Option to change whether the styles menu is horizontal (default) or vertical.


¹ globally; in any application

Body:

| Package | Change | Age | Adoption | Passing | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [com.google.android.material:material](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android) | `1.5.0` -> `1.11.0` | [![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/maven/com.google.android.material:material/1.11.0?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![adoption](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/adoption/maven/com.google.android.material:material/1.11.0?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![passing](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/compatibility/maven/com.google.android.material:material/1.5.0/1.11.0?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/maven/com.google.android.material:material/1.5.0/1.11.0?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>material-components/material-components-android (com.google.android.material:material)</summary>

### [`v1.11.0`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/releases/tag/1.11.0)

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/compare/1.10.0...1.11.0)

### Important

Elevation Overlays within default component styles have been replaced by the new [Tonal Surface Color system](https://material.io/blog/tone-based-surface-color-m3). Instead of blending the Primary color with the Surface color based on an elevation value, components now use a specific Surface color role that can be more easily understood and customized.

Documentation for the affected components and which Surface color roles they use has been updated in https://github.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/2114a11378fd801b32cd7431bb8a2c94de4f462c.

### What's new since 1.10.0

-   New [Tonal Surface Color system](https://material.io/blog/tone-based-surface-color-m3)! Check out the [design guidance](https://material.io/blog/tone-based-surface-color-m3) and the [commit with documentation updates](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/2114a11378fd801b32cd7431bb8a2c94de4f462c) for more details.
-   New Carousel variants! 1.11.0 introduces three new Carousel variants: center-aligned hero, uncontained, and fullscreen. Check out the [Carousel documentation](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/blob/master/docs/components/Carousel.md) for more details.

#### Dependency Updates

None.

*Note: Earlier versions of 1.11 used other versions of the `androidx.activity` 1.8.0 library dependency, but there is no change in the dependency from the last stable MDC library version (1.10.0).*

#### Library Updates

-   `Badging`
    -   Adjusted badge vertical offset for certain components to fit design requirements ([`78cc54b`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/78cc54b8c39da437732ff3d7c96de8f345e09348))
    -   Added note in attachBadgeDrawable method docs about menu item views being re-used when there is a structural change in the menu. Since badges are attached to the views, this may change the menu item that the badge is intended for ([`5e6ea58`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/5e6ea582801ebfe2af8457261581a2ad43c8051a))
-   `BottomNavigationView`
    -   Set badges to be null instead of removing them from the sparse array so that removing badges before restoring badge states will not override the current state ([`9f2e686`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/9f2e6864d2170d633709e7aa66b9e8e178352ef0))
-   `BottomSheet`
    -   Fix sheet corners animation ([`de27132`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/de271320e4ba046c73e35e18f687ee4637f3a6ac))
-   `Carousel`
    -   Ensure that extra small size is not larger than the large size ([`17baf71`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/17baf71972016cf4608d9c83b902bb2debee585b))
    -   Fixed crash when recyclerview has a size of zero. ([`26c3129`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/26c31292012868d7e9889f6ecee7559c61caa5b2))
    -   When navigating with keyboard, scroll focused item to nearest focal keyline, not the first focal keyline ([`fb9c1c6`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/fb9c1c6edfbdbe4791043a128c03a7fad5fd99f0))
    -   Force hero strategy to be start-aligned if there are not enough items to make it center-aligned ([`9a2347b`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/9a2347bda5312dd6dc3ab7818a6f71297dcf018b))
    -   Add logic for multibrowse strategy to change strategy when number of items is less than the number of keylines ([`cbb380d`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/cbb380df61f8eb66273043b437582c8058df7088))
    -   Fixed focus order when using keyboard navigation for hero carousels. ([`0356f24`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/0356f24a63b5c47ee23cbf5c0fca4d817e4d6acd))
    -   Cleaning up multi-browse strategy and removing compact arrangement ([`ed4647d`](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/commit/ed4647d5dfa42876063521546de2dbcf71914e01))

### What's new since 1.9.0

-   Added Predictive back support for search, bottom sheet, side sheet and navigation drawer. Check out the [developer documentation](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/blob/master/docs/foundations/PredictiveBack.md) for more details.
-   Add `Start-aligned` variant to Carousel component. Check out the [developer documentation](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/blob/master/docs/components/Carousel.md) for more details.
-   Badge component updates. Check out the [developer documentation](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/blob/master/docs/components/BadgeDrawable.md) for more details.
-   Left & RTL side sheets. Check out the [developer documentation](https://togithub.com/material-components/material-components-android/blob/master/docs/components/SideSheet.md) for more details.

### [`v7.6.3`](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/releases/tag/v7.6.3): 7.6.3

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/compare/v7.6.2...v7.6.3)

This is a patch release for 7.6. We recommend using 7.6.3 instead of 7.6.

This release addresses two security vulnerabilities:

-   [Incorrect permission assignment for symlinked files used in copy or archiving operations](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/security/advisories/GHSA-43r3-pqhv-f7h9)
-   [Possible local text file exfiltration by XML External entity injection](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/security/advisories/GHSA-mrff-q8qj-xvg8)

It also fixes the following issues:

-   [#​25781](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/25781) Backport finalized task performance fix to 7.6.x
-   [#​25802](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/25802) Backport cgroups fix to 7.6.x

[Read the Release Notes](https://docs.gradle.org/7.6.3/release-notes.html)

#### Upgrade Instructions

Switch your build to use Gradle 7.6.3 by updating your wrapper:

    ./gradlew wrapper --gradle-version=7.6.3

See the [Gradle 7.x upgrade guide](https://docs.gradle.org/7.6.3/userguide/upgrading_version\_7.html#changes\_7.6) to learn about deprecations, breaking changes and other considerations when upgrading to Gradle 7.6.3.

#### Reporting Problems

If you're not sure you're encountering a bug, please use the [forum](https://discuss.gradle.org/c/help-discuss).

### [`v7.6.2`](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/releases/tag/v7.6.2): 7.6.2

[Compare Source](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/compare/v7.6.1...v7.6.2)

This is a patch release for 7.6. We recommend using 7.6.2 instead of 7.6.

This release addresses two security vulnerabilities:

-   [Dependency cache path traversal](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/security/advisories/GHSA-2h6c-rv6q-494v)
-   [Path traversal vulnerabilities in handling of Tar archives](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/security/advisories/GHSA-84mw-qh6q-v842)

It also fixes the following issues:

-   [#​23201](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23201) Backport dependency upgrades to 7.x
-   [#​23202](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23202) Backport Scala incremental compilation fixes
-   [#​23325](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23325) Backport JSoup update to resolve CVE-2022-36033
-   [#​23458](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23458) Backport JUnit5 dynamic test logging bug fix
-   [#​23681](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23681) Dependency graph resolution: Equivalent excludes can cause un-necessary graph mutations \[backport 7.x]
-   [#​23922](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23922) Backport "Use Compiler API data for incremental compilation after a failure" to 7.x
-   [#​23951](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23951) Exclude rule merging: missing optimization \[Backport 7.x]
-   [#​24132](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/24132) Extending an already resolved configuration no longer works correctly \[backport 7.x]
-   [#​24234](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/24234) 7.6.1 breaks gradle-consistent-versions
-   [#​24390](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/24390) Gradle 7.4 fails on multi release jar's with JDK 19 code
-   [#​24439](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/24439) Gradle complains about invalid tool chain - picking up the source package location - it should just ignore them \[Backport]
-   [#​24443](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/24443) Maven artifact referenced only in dependency constraints raises IllegalStateException: Corrupt serialized resolution result \[backport]
-   [#​24901](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/24901) Backport fix for test exception that cannot be deserialized to 7.x

[Read the Release Notes](https://docs.gradle.org/7.6.2/release-notes.html)

### [`v7.6.1`](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/releases/tag/v7.6.1): 7.6.1

-   [#​23074](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23074) Docs: Build Lifecycle starts halfway through a point
-   [#​23096](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23096) Classifiers of version catalog are discarded while copied to anothor dependency
-   [#​23111](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23111) Ant closures are broken with Gradle 7.6

-   [#​23294](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23294) "Unable to make progress running work" together with --continue and failing tasks (Backport to 7.6.1)

-   [#​23894](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23894) Update EOL policy
-   [#​23910](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23910) Backport trusting only full GPG keys in dependency verification \[Backport 7.6.1]
-   [#​23941](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23941) Typo in v7.6 docs about disabling_the_daemon
-   [#​23985](https://togithub.com/gradle/gradle/issues/23985) Resolving of manually created configuration creates a ResolveException

Title: Some letters have the wrong fonts
Body:
**Describe the bug**
Some letters have the wrong fonts

**To Reproduce**
Select the following fonts, and type the alphabet
* Italic serif:
𝑎𝑏𝑐𝑑𝑒𝑓𝑔 ℎ 𝑖𝑗𝑘𝑙𝑚𝑛𝑜𝑝𝑞𝑟𝑠𝑡𝑢𝑣𝑤𝑥𝑦𝑧
The h does not have serifs, it's only italic. Comparing it with the one from Bold Italic Serif makes it even more obvious:
𝒂𝒃𝒄𝒅𝒆𝒇𝒈 𝒉 𝒊𝒋𝒌𝒍𝒎𝒏𝒐𝒑𝒒𝒓𝒔𝒕𝒖𝒗𝒘𝒙𝒚𝒛

* Script:
𝒶𝒷𝒸𝒹ℯ𝒻ℊ𝒽𝒾𝒿𝓀𝓁𝓂𝓃ℴ𝓅𝓆𝓇𝓈𝓉𝓊𝓋𝓌𝓍𝓎𝓏
When I type then compare this g with the one from Bold Script, they look different, the one from Script looks like it's only italic
𝓪𝓫𝓬𝓭𝓮𝓯𝓰𝓱𝓲𝓳𝓴𝓵𝓶𝓷𝓸𝓹𝓺𝓻𝓼𝓽𝓾𝓿𝔀𝔁𝔂𝔃
But somehow the page preview doesn't show this mistake like other software do, so here's a picture
![Screenshot_20210517-145232_1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/58000526/118500460-b5638700-b71f-11eb-973c-7155b1f30e7b.jpg)

* Organic uppercase:
ᗩᗷᑕᗪᗴᖴᘜᕼIᒍKᒪᗰᑎOᑭᑫᖇՏԎᑌᐯᗯ᙭Yᘔ
the K and Y are not following the font, they are just uppercase K and Y.

* supertiny:
ᵃᵇᶜᵈᵉᶠᵍʰᶦʲᵏˡᵐⁿᵒᵖᵠʳˢᵗᵘᵛʷˣʸᶻ
ᴬᴮᶜᴰᴱᶠᴳᴴᴵᴶᴷᴸᴹᴺᴼᴾᵠᴿˢᵀᵁⱽᵂˣʸᶻ
The letters c, f, q, s, v, x, y, z are only in lowercase and the q doesn't look right.

**Expected behavior**
The g from the Script font always looking the way it should just like the other letters do.
The wrong previously mentioned letters in the fonts remplaced with the right ones.

**Android version**
Android 8.0 "Oreo."

Thank you.

Title: Secondary key actions
Body:
**Received this feedback**

> It would be nice to have enhanced long-press options as e.g. special characters.  Long pressing g for example is dead but could produce a & as in other keyboards. Changing the time a long-press takes would also be desired and easy to make real. (User defined slider from say 0 to 500ms instead of a fixed value x). I really like the privacy concept and there is no problem with no autocorrect. One could start a sentence with capital letters tho.

- make long press time customizable (opened separate issue) → #88 
- (duplicate) capitalize sentences → #59

Body:

In some styles (e.g. 𝓉𝒽𝒾𝓈) chars are longer to the right than the actuel character border. This results in a cutted letter in some cases(e.g. in the whatsapp status or even the "style priview- coosing- bar" in the keyboard itself)

Solution:
Add the Char U+2800 to the keyboard. It is blank and gives the opportunity to insert it after the text manually if the last char is cutted, so it isnt. Maybe it would fit after longprssing the period?

Title: Non-persistent clipboard history
Body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Copy some text to have a clipboard history 
2. Launch the keyboard settings app and close it
3. Now check the clipboard history (by long press clipboard icon)

Note: Clipboard also clears when switching keyboard 

### Expected behaviour
Clipboard history should be there

### Actual behaviour
Clipboard is empty, there is nothing to paste

**Android OS version:**
Android 11

Title: Simultaneous press of key and space causes reversed order of characters
Body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Choose any of two Minimalist !with! emoji at UI->Common bottom generic row
2. Press any letter, hold it, and before long press happens, press space

### Expected behaviour
the characters should appear in the order of downpress events: [letter][space]


### Actual behaviour
The order of the characters is reversed: [space][letter] happens

this behaviour is always present, regardless of theme or language or long pres stime or multi-tap timeout
but it isn't present when using other generic bottom row including Minimal (no emoji)
doesn't happen if the second key is different (any letter or question mark, period, comma from bottom row)


**Android OS version:**
Realme UI 4.0 | Android 13
RMX2202_13.1.0.1810(EX01)

**Device manufacturer and model:**
realme GT 5G

**List of installed add-ons (like languages, or themes):**
Lean Dark Option 2 is current

Title: en dvorak: add ð & þ
Body:
Eth & thorn, ð & þ, were historic letters to English & still common in Icelandic words. There seems to be recurring movements online to use them in English today too to supplant the most common digraph, “th”, for [θ] [ð] sounds.

Placement was at the beginning of the list as these characters have higher codepoint values than the others (altho the order doesn’t have an order I personally understand—and ideally users could reorder them).

Title: Hebrew uses a deprecated language code
Body:
In situations where the language code is displayed (such as the word editor and the next word settings), AnySoftKeyboard uses the old, deprecated-in-1989 language code `iw` rather than the modern `he`.
![Screenshot_20231226-194317_AnySoftKeyboard](https://github.com/AnySoftKeyboard/AnySoftKeyboard/assets/109424320/8561995b-efb8-4351-8022-34fc1a27796c)

Interestingly, Indonesian, which was deprecated the same year, does use the correct code:
![Screenshot_20231226-195140_AnySoftKeyboard](https://github.com/AnySoftKeyboard/AnySoftKeyboard/assets/109424320/c9858319-b959-4412-8501-a11c8365d509)

From [this comment](https://github.com/tuskyapp/Tusky/issues/2903#issuecomment-1323741899) on another project that had that issue, I suspect that whoever made the Indonesian addon used proper [ISO](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_639) while the maker of the Hebrew addon used Android's locale list and that's it, which is strangely out-of-date.

Title: ANR when typing after selecting link
Body:
### Steps to reproduce
1. Install [this notetaking app](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/com.maltaisn.notes.sync/) from F-droid. [This](https://f-droid.org/repo/com.maltaisn.notes.sync_10503.apk) is the apk if you prefer a direct link.
2. Press the `+` button at the bottom right, to make a note.
3. Type `example.org   hello` as the note content (below the title).
4. Press ASK's left arrow. This selects the link.
   4.1. The fact that the link is selected is reported [here](https://github.com/maltaisn/another-notes-app/issues/127), but I think that ASK should not give an ANR in any case.
5. While the link is selected,
5.1. if you type `(` then ASK freezes and there is the ANR `Reason: Input dispatching timed out (Waiting to send non-key event because the touched window has not finished processing certain input events that were delivered to it over 500.0ms ago.  Wait queue length: 14.  Wait queue head age: 79937.5ms.)`
5.2. if you have typed a letter instead of `(`, it has replaced the link text, as expected.
7. To restart ASK, I open another app, focus a text box, wait a few seconds.

This happens with Italian and German language packs on ASK 1.11.7137 + Samsung Android 11, Samsung Android 7.1.

(edited for clarity)

Title: Feature-include impending closure of bracket/quote
Body:
It is tedious to have to do twice (so I manually do this), please allow **auto closure** of brackets, quotes, tags and other paired symbols.
• When user presses ( or ‘or “ or [ or { or < or « key,
• Place ) or ” or ’ or ] or } or > or » ahead of cursor (allow math people disable < action).
• When a second space is pressed the cursor moves to outside of closure object (maybe one for tags).

- Some people use nested brackets and quotes, permit piling them up in closure sequence beyond cursor. possessive (both’ place’s) and abbreviation apostrophes are closing types and will not interfere if system responds only in the defined ways (’til and ’90s). I like to put commas after brackets (as here), so keyboard's existing comma/period move function needs to be applied too.

Example: Cursor shall be |
• Trigger symbol added, next placed ahead.
He said "|"
• Continue typing
He said "Quickly|"
• One space still continues
He said "Quickly! |"
• Second space closes (any proceeding space temporarily absorbed, maybe temporarily shuttle any proceeding punctuation too)
He said "Quickly!" |
• Comma shuttled across when pressed (subsequent space press could be ignored)
He said "Quickly!", |
N.B. keyboard theme hinders "/ “ / ” recognition, left mistake present.

Title: [feature request] Non-Breaking Space
Body:
Hi devs and everybody,

It would be a good feature to be able to make non-breaking spaces with the keyboard.

In french, it is required for big punctuation: before `:`, `;`, `!`, `?`, `»` and after  `«`.
But I think it could be useful for anybody who wants to keep a text in shape. It is usable in several cases:

- for dates:                                                                           November 9th 2023
- before currency unit symbols:                 100 $, 100 €
- before measurement unit symbols: 11 feet, 200 km, 60 kg, 25 kN
- before percent symbol:                                    90 %
- before degree symbol:                                      20 °C, 68 °F
- as a thousands separator:                             1 000 000

Non-breaking spaces allow us that prevents an automatic line break at its position and keeps the text consistent.
